_meta.name: tux
_meta.description: Design tokens for tti-ux. Ships TTI as the default institutional theme; other TAMUS themes extend the base.

maroon.$description: Primary brand anchor. Sampled from tti-overview + factsheet; slightly darker than PPTX #5C0825, both are in TTI's accepted range.

maroon-deep.$description: High-contrast maroon from 508-accessible PPTX. Use for critical UI where AA contrast is non-negotiable.

gold.$description: Primary accent. TTI signature warm-ochre gold — not bright Aggie yellow.

navy.$description: Secondary accent. Used in option-1 PPTX and on infographic sections.

teal.$description: Tertiary / category color. Use sparingly, for data-viz category differentiation.

charcoal.$description: Body copy. Not pure black — softer, matches print collateral.

tti-hc._description: High-contrast theme, derived from the 508-accessible PPTX. For users who opt in, and as our WCAG AAA audit reference.

tti-dark._description: Dark theme. Warm-charcoal surfaces (not pure black) so the editorial palette feels continuous with the light theme. Brand primary shifts from maroon to lifted TTI teal (#6BB4C0) on dark surfaces because lightened-maroon variants read pink against the small chrome elements where the accent lives (composer borders, focus rings, active-tab text). Maroon brand presence is preserved via `brand.fill` for marketing panels and via chart-1 pinned to wine so the primary chart series keeps the maroon association. Every semantic + chart + map value here targets AAA (7:1) on the dark page surface; numbers verified by /contrast-audit. See palette.md § Dark theme for the full rationale.

primary.$description: Lifted TTI teal (base {color.tti.teal} #4A8892 lifted for readability on dark). 7.1:1 on raised, ~8.2:1 on page, ~8.7:1 on sunken.

accent.$description: Gold accent unchanged on dark — its job is emphasis, not legibility.

fill.$description: Marketing panel fill — kept dark wine in all themes so white text on top passes contrast.

page.$description: Warm charcoal page — not pure black.

semantic._description: All target AAA (7:1) on the dark page surface #15100F. Lifted compared to the base semantic palette — sage → mint, gold → pale wheat, maroon → pale rose, teal → pale cyan.

focus._description: Two-ring focus on dark — outer is the lifted-teal brand primary, inner is a deep warm-gold halo so the ring reads against both raised and sunken surfaces.

chart._description: Categorical palette lifted ~15% to read against the warm-charcoal page. chart-1 is pinned to wine-rose rather than brand.primary so the primary chart series keeps the maroon brand association even though brand.primary resolves to teal in dark mode.

map._description: Choropleth ramps lifted for the warm-charcoal page. Maroon ramp reads as a warm gradient (deep maroon → light salmon); slate goes from near-charcoal up to near-white. Outline alpha boosted so state shapes stay visible on the dark page.

tamu._description: Texas A&M University — verified brand colors. Maroon 500000 is TAMU's canonical anchor.

pvamu._description: Prairie View A&M — PLACEHOLDER values. Confirm with PVAMU Marketing & Communications before releasing a PVAMU-branded build.

tarleton._description: Tarleton State — PLACEHOLDER values. Confirm with Tarleton brand team.

wtamu._description: West Texas A&M — PLACEHOLDER values. Confirm with WTAMU brand team.

sans.$value: "Public Sans", "Franklin Gothic ATF", "Franklin Gothic", "Helvetica Neue", Arial, sans-serif

display.$value: "Public Sans", "Franklin Gothic ATF", "Franklin Gothic", "Helvetica Neue", Arial, sans-serif

breakpoint._description: Layout breakpoints. Values match Tailwind v4 defaults so `md:` / `lg:` utilities keep their intuitive meaning, but the SEMANTIC trigger for each breakpoint is documented here — what surface state changes at this width. Survey reference: Microsoft Windows UI kit responsive ladder (348/660/708/1020/1252), rebased to TUX's editorial-first content widths.

xs.$description: Mobile portrait. Single column, full-bleed cards, side rail (sidebar layout) is hidden behind a hamburger overlay.

sm.$description: Large phone / small tablet portrait. Still single column; touch targets enlarge, body type goes up half a step.

md.$description: Tablet. Two-column layouts viable; side rail still collapsed by default, accessible via hamburger.

lg.$description: Small laptop. Side rail expands to icon-only mode. Data-dense surfaces begin showing right-side context panels.

xl.$description: Standard laptop. Rail icons gain labels; editorial column hits its max-width (~72ch) with comfortable side margins.

2xl.$description: Wide desktop. Optional right-side context panel slot opens. Data-dense surfaces reach natural density.
